title: Forgetful causal masking makes causal language models better zero-shot learners

structured.summary: The paper proposes Perplection, a perplexity-based method for zero-shot prompt template selection that requires no labeled data or development set. By hypothesizing that lower perplexity of prompted inputs correlates with better zero-shot classification performance, the method selects templates minimizing language discrepancy with pretraining data. Experiments across multiple datasets and languages demonstrate that Perplection reliably outperforms random selection and competes with methods relying on annotated dev sets.

strengths.point: Addresses practical zero-shot prompt selection without labeled data
strengths.evidence: Perplection enables annotation-free template ranking, bridging the gap between theoretical zero-shot setups and realistic usage scenarios.

strengths.point: Simple yet effective method leveraging perplexity as a proxy for language discrepancy
strengths.evidence: Empirical results show a strong negative correlation between template perplexity and zero-shot accuracy, validated on Chinese and English datasets.

weaknesses.point: Limited novelty due to omission of prior work on perplexity-based domain adaptation and data selection
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not cite foundational works such as Moore and Lewis (2010) or Axelrod et al. (2011) that also leverage perplexity for data-model fit or domain similarity.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient analysis on impact of label word choice and perplexity normalization
weaknesses.evidence: The method fills mask tokens with candidate label words without ablation on label word selection; perplexity is unnormalized by template length, risking bias toward shorter prompts.

questions.question: How robust is the correlation between perplexity and zero-shot accuracy across diverse tasks and more complex prompt types?
questions.motivation: Understanding robustness is crucial to generalize Perplection beyond classification with discrete labels.

questions.question: Can Perplection be effectively extended to decoder-only or encoder-decoder LLMs and continuous prompt tuning?
questions.motivation: Most state-of-the-art zero-shot methods use large LLMs; applicability to these models would enhance practical relevance.

critique_points.point_001: Limited novelty due to omission of prior work on perplexity-based domain adaptation and data selection. The paper does not cite foundational works such as Moore and Lewis (2010) or Axelrod et al. (2011) that also leverage perplexity for data-model fit or domain similarity
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient analysis on impact of label word choice and perplexity normalization. The method fills mask tokens with candidate label words without ablation on label word selection; perplexity is unnormalized by template length, risking bias toward shorter prompts
